title: Jamie Dimon Says He Has Seen the Numbers and SpaceX’s Orbital Data Centers ‘Could Actually Work’ Despite Technical, Valuation Risks
excerpt: JPMorgan Chase & Co. CEO Jamie Dimon on Monday called Starlink an "extraordinary product" and said SpaceX’s plan to build artificial-intelligence data center...
content_html:
Benzinga and Yahoo Finance LLC may earn commission or revenue on some items through the links below.

JPMorgan Chase & Co. CEO Jamie Dimon on Monday called Starlink an "extraordinary product" and said SpaceX's plan to build artificial-intelligence data centers in orbit could work, offering a prominent Wall Street endorsement as the newly public company's shares hover near their IPO price.

Dimon Sees Orbital Computing's Economic Potential

Speaking on "The Master Investor Podcast with Wilfred Frost," Dimon called SpaceX "an extraordinary company" after visiting its operations. JPMorgan helped market SpaceX's record $75 billion IPO, which priced at $135 per share in June.

Don't Miss:

  • A single bad hire can set a startup back years. Here are the 5 hires founders most often misjudge — and why

  • Still Learning the Market? These 50 Must-Know Terms Can Help You Catch Up Fast

"I went to visit it, and Starlink is an extraordinary product," Dimon said. "I've seen numbers on the data centers in space that could actually work."

Dimon said orbital computing could tap cheap solar power, reduce cooling expenses and avoid some constraints facing ground-based facilities. He acknowledged difficulties in returning data to Earth, but said laser links could redirect transmissions between satellites when clouds block a receiving site.

Trending: Avoid the #1 Investing Mistake: How Your 'Safe' Holdings Could Be Costing You Big Time

Technical And Commercial Risks Remain Unresolved

SpaceX operates more than 10,000 Starlink satellites, the world's largest low-Earth-orbit communications network. Elon Musk plans to test orbital AI computing by the end of 2027 using technology derived largely from Starlink V3 spacecraft. Musk has called solar-powered orbital data centers a "no-brainer," and recently said SpaceX could fly the first systems next year.

The concept remains unproven. SpaceX warned in its IPO filing that orbital data centers may never become commercially viable. Amazon Web Services CEO Matt Garman called the idea "pretty far" from reality, citing launch capacity and payload costs.

Investors Debate Valuation As Shares Retreat

Investors remain divided. Deepwater Management Managing Partner Gene Munster has argued that SpaceX's rockets, satellites and AI stack create a "sovereign AI" advantage that competitors cannot easily match. The Future Fund LLC's Gary Black has focused on valuation, saying investors should not pay roughly 150 times projected 2026 EBITDA.

See Also: Skip the Regrets: The Essential Retirement Tips Experts Wish Everyone Knew Earlier.

content_html_zh:
Benzinga 和 Yahoo Finance LLC 可能通过以下链接赚取某些项目的佣金或收入。

摩根大通公司 首席执行官杰米·戴蒙 (Jamie Dimon) 周一称 Starlink 是一款“非凡的产品”，并表示 SpaceX 在轨道上建立人工智能数据中心的计划可能会奏效，这家新上市公司的股价徘徊在 IPO 价格附近，这为这家新上市公司提供了华尔街的认可。

戴蒙看到了轨道计算的经济潜力

戴蒙在参观了 SpaceX 的运营情况后，在“威尔弗雷德·弗罗斯特的投资大师播客”中发表讲话，称 SpaceX 是“一家非凡的公司”。 摩根大通帮助 SpaceX 完成了创纪录的 750 亿美元 IPO，6 月份的发行价为每股 135 美元。

不要错过：

  • 一次糟糕的招聘可能会让一家初创企业倒退数年。 以下是创始人最常误判的 5 名员工——以及原因
  • 还在学习市场吗？ 这 50 个必须知道的术语可以帮助您快速跟上

“我去参观了它，星链是一个非凡的产品，”戴蒙说。 “我已经看到了太空数据中心的实际运行情况。”

戴蒙表示，轨道计算可以利用廉价的太阳能，减少冷却费用，并避免地面设施面临的一些限制。 他承认将数据传回地球存在困难，但表示当云层遮挡接收地点时，激光链路可以重定向卫星之间的传输。

趋势：避免第一大投资错误：你的“安全”持仓可能会让你付出巨大的代价

技术和商业风险仍未解决

SpaceX 运营着 10,000 多颗 Starlink 卫星，这是世界上最大的低地球轨道通信网络。 埃隆·马斯克计划在 2027 年底之前使用主要源自 Starlink V3 航天器的技术来测试轨道人工智能计算。 马斯克称太阳能轨道数据中心是“理所当然的”，最近还表示 SpaceX 可能会在明年发射第一批系统。

这个概念尚未得到证实。 SpaceX 在 IPO 文件中警告称，轨道数据中心可能永远无法实现商业可行性。 亚马逊网络服务首席执行官马特·加曼称这个想法与现实“相去甚远”，并提到了发射能力和有效载荷成本。

随着股价下跌，投资者对估值争论不休

投资者仍存在分歧。 Deepwater Management 管理合伙人吉恩·蒙斯特 (Gene Munster) 认为，SpaceX 的火箭、卫星和人工智能堆栈创造了竞争对手无法轻易匹敌的“主权人工智能”优势。 未来基金有限责任公司 (Future Fund LLC) 的加里·布莱克 (Gary Black) 专注于估值，表示投资者不应支付 2026 年预计 EBITDA 约 150 倍的费用。

另请参阅：跳过遗憾：专家希望每个人都早点知道的基本退休技巧。
